message:
fix(browser-core): abort in-flight fetch when task is cancelled

When a task was cancelled via task_cancel(), the cancellation was
recorded in the dispatcher state but any in-flight browser fetch()
request initiated for that task continued running until completion or
network timeout. This wasted bandwidth, kept the JS event loop busy with
a request whose result would be discarded, and left the AbortController
allocated until natural completion.

Fix: after the dispatcher records the cancellation, look up any in-flight
fetch AbortController registered for the task ID via
take_inflight_fetch() and call .abort() on it. The wasm32 cfg-gate
ensures this code only compiles for the browser target where
AbortController exists.

The lookup uses take (not get) so the controller is removed from the
inflight registry in the same step, preventing duplicate aborts on
repeated cancel calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
